name: Do I need a lawyer for work chemical exposure claim or can I do it myself?
description: DIY possible but lawyer boosts payout 25%+ on contingency—no risk.

mainEntity.name: Do I need a lawyer for work chemical exposure claim or can I do it myself?

acceptedAnswer.text: DIY possible but lawyer boosts payout 25%+ on contingency—no risk.
acceptedAnswer.description: **You can file initially yourself, but a lawyer dramatically increases success odds, especially for denials or complex exposures.** Utah and Idaho workers' comp is adversarial; insurers contest chemical claims rigorously.[1][6] Key factors: no upfront fees (contingency), higher payouts (up to 25% more), and handling appeals/paperwork. Common mistakes like early settlements cost thousands; lawyers negotiate better. Most offer free consults—use one if symptoms persist.
